slides.title: Analytics & Reports
slides.subtitle: Turn platform data into actionable business insights

slides.title: Reporting in Kissflow
bullets: Reports available for processes, dataforms, and boards
Visual report builder with drag-and-drop field selection
Multiple visualization types: table, chart, pivot, KPI cards
Filter, group, sort, and aggregate data
Export to CSV, Excel, and PDF
Schedule automated email delivery to stakeholders

slides.title: Creating a Report
bullets: Open flow editor → Reports tab → Create new report
Select data source fields from form and system metadata
Choose report type: Tabular, Bar, Line, Pie, Pivot, KPI
Apply filters: date range, status, user, department, custom fields
Configure grouping — group by field with aggregate functions
Preview report with live data before saving
slides.notes: Demo building a simple tabular report step by step.

slides.title: Report Visualization Types
bullets: Tabular — rows and columns, sortable and filterable
Bar chart — compare categories side by side
Line chart — trends over time periods
Pie/Donut chart — proportional breakdown of a whole
Pivot table — cross-tabulate two or more dimensions
KPI card — single metric with optional comparison to target
slides.notes: Choose visualization that best communicates the insight.

slides.title: Filters, Grouping, and Aggregation
bullets: Filter by any form field, system field, or workflow metadata
Date filters: today, this week, this month, custom range
Group by field — department, status, category, assignee
Aggregates: COUNT, SUM, AVG, MIN, MAX on numeric fields
Multiple grouping levels for drill-down analysis
Save filter presets for reusable report configurations
slides.notes: Show grouped report: submissions per department per month.

slides.title: AI Report Creation
bullets: Describe the report you need in natural language
AI selects fields, filters, grouping, and visualization type
Refine generated report in visual builder
Accelerates report creation for business users
Works across process, dataform, and board data sources
Enterprise AI with constraint-aware output

slides.title: Report Permissions
bullets: Report Viewer role — access reports without process participation
Separate from process initiator/participant permissions
Assign report access to managers, executives, auditors
Row-level security inherited from source flow permissions
Embed reports in apps with app role-based visibility
Export permissions configurable per report
slides.notes: Executives often need report access without being workflow participants.

slides.title: Report Scheduling
bullets: Schedule reports: daily, weekly, monthly, or custom cron
Email report as PDF or CSV attachment to recipient list
Include report snapshot — data as of schedule execution time
Multiple schedules per report for different audiences
Monitor schedule execution in admin logs
Pause or modify schedules without deleting the report
slides.notes: Demo scheduling a weekly status report to team leads.

slides.title: Dashboards in Apps
bullets: Combine multiple report/chart components on one app page
Create role-specific dashboards — ops, management, executive
KPI cards at top, detail charts below, tabular data at bottom
Auto-refresh or manual refresh for live data
Responsive layout for desktop and mobile viewing
Dashboard as default landing page for app users
slides.notes: Show executive dashboard with 4-5 chart components.

slides.title: Admin Analytics
bullets: Platform-level analytics for administrators
Track app usage, active users, and adoption trends
Process volume and completion rate metrics
Identify unused or underutilized apps and processes
User activity monitoring for security and compliance
Source: community.kissflow.com > Admin Analytics
slides.notes: IT admins use this for platform health monitoring.

slides.title: Export and External BI
bullets: Export any report to CSV or Excel for offline analysis
API access to report data for Power BI, Tableau, Looker
Scheduled exports to cloud storage via integrations
Large dataset exports with pagination support
Maintain data freshness with scheduled API pulls
Combine Kissflow data with other enterprise data sources
slides.notes: For organizations with existing BI infrastructure.

slides.title: Analytics Best Practices
bullets: Define KPIs before building reports — what decisions will this inform?
Create operational reports (daily) and strategic reports (monthly)
Use consistent naming conventions across reports
Test reports with realistic data volumes before go-live
Review and prune unused reports quarterly
Train report viewers on how to interpret and act on data
